rationale_overall: Quiet quarter-end at the cap=8 ceiling: every holding's Q3 2025 calendar-quarter print or Q4 announcement firmed the thesis, and no external candidate cleared the bar to displace a current name. NVDA's Nov 19 Q3 FY26 report set a record $57.0B in revenue with $51.2B data-center (up 25% sequentially) and Jensen calling Blackwell 'sold out'; ISRG's Oct 21 Q3 placed 240 da Vinci 5 systems vs 110 a year ago with 20% worldwide procedure growth; LLY's Oct 30 Q3 grew worldwide revenue 54% on Mounjaro +109% and Zepbound +185%, with orforglipron submitted for obesity by year-end after four positive Phase 3 readouts; LNG's Q3 produced ~$1.6B adjusted EBITDA on record European LNG demand (+26% YoY imports) as the EU 2027 Russian-gas exit firmed bilateral offtakes; the nuclear bucket got fatter with Vistra's 20-year 1.2 GW Amazon deal and the Meta multi-vendor 6.6 GW SMR award announced in Q4, keeping NLR's broad-basket exposure well-positioned; QTUM held its lead on IonQ's #AQ 64 milestone three months ahead of schedule with 99.99% two-qubit gate fidelity. The only mild dent was RKLB's Nov 10 Q3 call publicly slipping Neutron from 'end of 2025' to Q1 2026 ship with first launch thereafter — a quarter's slip on a major program, not a thesis breach, especially with the company guiding to record 2025 revenue and $1.85B backlog. MSFT's hyperscale AI tailwind remains intact via Azure capex and OpenAI partnership. With 8 of 8 slots earning their keep and the cap forcing any add to pair with a remove, the right move is hold and let the optimizer re-weight.
